evals.prompt: My MEMORY.md is over its size limit and has duplicate entries. /compact-memory
evals.expected_output: Runs the analyzer, previews a plan that merges duplicates and shrinks the index under both the 200-line and 25KB limits, waits for approval before writing, then reports a measured before>after index size.
Only obvious duplicates and explicitly-SUPERSEDED entries are retired; near-duplicates and stale-by-date entries are left in place.
No files are retired or merged; only long index lines are shortened (detail relocated into topic files) to bring MEMORY.md under budget.
Entries about dev tickets / evidence are protected from retirement even if they look stale; other consolidation proceeds.

evals.prompt: A memory file has malformed YAML frontmatter. /compact-memory
evals.expected_output: The malformed file is surfaced in the plan (not silently dropped); the run continues and the file is preserved.

evals.prompt: My memory store is already small and clean. /compact-memory
evals.expected_output: Reports that the store is already under budget with nothing to consolidate (no-op), optionally fixing any orphan pointers or broken links.

evals.prompt: After /compact-memory, confirm nothing was permanently lost.
evals.expected_output: Retired files are present under the sibling memory-archive/ with tombstones; a pre-run snapshot exists; nothing was hard-deleted.

evals.prompt: After /compact-memory archived some entries, verify they cannot bleed back into recall.
evals.expected_output: The structural guarantee holds: retired files live in the sibling memory-archive/ (outside memory/) AND their MEMORY.md pointers are removed, so neither the directory scan nor the index can surface them. The model may note that an optional one-time cross-session sentinel check (a marker in the archive that must never appear in a fresh session's recall) is described in the plugin README.
